commit.message:
Revert Newtonsoft.Json dependency tracking (#15596) (#15651)

Reverts the assembly resolution tracking and warning mechanism for
Newtonsoft.Json that was added in #15596. Investigation showed that:

- vstest's AssemblyResolver.OnResolve never fires for Newtonsoft.Json
  because it is loaded via normal CLR probing before the event triggers
- On .NET Framework, MSTest has its own AssemblyResolver in a separate
  AppDomain that resolves NJ independently from vstest's resolver
- The ProvidedDependencies telemetry was always empty in practice
- No feasible proactive warning mechanism exists for this scenario

The [DoNotParallelize] test fixes from the same PR are preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Direct VPC Egress on UI + use API service URI from data source

- Add vpc_access Direct VPC Egress (ALL_TRAFFIC) to UI Cloud Run so the
  nginx BFF's requests to the API are routed through the project VPC and
  treated as internal by Cloud Run's ingress checker.  This unblocks
  INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_ONLY on the API without a VPC connector.

- Use data.google_cloud_run_v2_service.api.uri for API_URL instead of a
  hand-computed URL string.

The 'default: bins other = default;' syntax confused Xcelium's parser —
the 'default:' looked like a case label. Changed to just

commit.message:
feat: add Direct VPC Egress so UI can reach internal-only API

Without VPC egress, Cloud Run-to-Cloud Run requests in the same project
are routed through the public internet and rejected as external traffic
by INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_ONLY (404).

Adding Direct VPC Egress with egress=ALL_TRAFFIC routes the UI container's
outbound requests through the project's default VPC.  Cloud Run then
recognises those requests as intra-VPC (internal) traffic, satisfying the
API's ingress restriction without a Serverless VPC Access Connector.

ALL_TRAFFIC (not PRIVATE_RANGES_ONLY) is required because the API's
*.run.app hostname resolves to a public IP; only ALL_TRAFFIC sends that
through the VPC path so Cloud Run treats it as internal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove cbodnar.com CNAME — serve from randubin.github.io/ByteDefend

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Demo DB schema + uptime monitor

Root cause: init.sql never ran on Neon — tables were missing.
Manually initialized Neon DB (all 63 tables now exist).

Fixes:
- docker/init.sql: Move hospital_patient_invites after hospital_networks
  (was forward reference — dependency didn't exist yet when run stmt-by-stmt)
- docker/init.sql: Quote 'current_role' column in role_change_requests
  (PostgreSQL reserved keyword — caused syntax error preventing table creation)
- backend/src/routes/index.ts: Quote current_role in all SQL queries
- backend/src/utils/database.ts: Quote current_role in CREATE TABLE safety net
- render-start.sh: SEED_ON_STARTUP only seeds if DB empty (not force-reseed).
  FORCE_RESEED=true is the only way to force a re-seed.
- .github/workflows/uptime-monitor.yml: New workflow — pings both Demo and Dev
  every 5 minutes to prevent Render/Neon free-tier spin-down

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deploy: 更新博客内容 2026-04-10 21:31:29

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: ensure vuln files are cached and re-extracted when missing

Two fixes to ensure per-arch vulnerability JSON files are always
available for the dashboard links:

1. fetch_suse_registry_images.py: tighten cache invalidation for
   container images - force re-extraction when:
   - vulnerabilities.source != 'attestation' (old Trivy data)
   - details_files is absent (migration from older attestation run)
   - any per-arch vuln file is missing from disk (cache cleared)

2. .github/workflows/static.yml: add vulns/ to the cache restore/save
   paths alongside sboms/, so per-arch vuln attestation files persist
   between workflow runs and are deployed to GitHub Pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Deploy: 更新博客内容 2026-04-10 21:28:57

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(vip): increase default inter_conv_gap to 400ns for 2-context MPTDC

The MPTDC has N_CTX=2 contexts. With the old 50ns gap, a 3rd conversion
arrives while both contexts are still measuring, causing the async frontend
to reject it (any_ctx_free=0). 400ns ensures at least one context frees
before the next conversion starts.

Also adds optional gap_ps parameter to gen_tdc_conversions for tests
that need explicit control.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: branded login UX, improved manifest & tools, timeouts, Edge account picker fix (#3)

* feat: custom MSAL loopback client with branded login landing page

Replace MSAL's default loopback server with LoginLoopbackClient that
serves a branded landing page with 'Sign in with Microsoft' button,
handles the OAuth redirect, and shows a success page with auto-close
countdown.

Changes:
- src/loopback.ts: LoginLoopbackClient implementing ILoopbackClient
  - Landing page at / with Microsoft sign-in button
  - /redirect endpoint does 302 to Microsoft auth URL
  - Auth code capture with redirect to /done (strips code from history)
  - Success page with countdown timer and auto-close
  - Error page with escaped error messages
- src/auth.ts: MsalAuthenticator now accepts openBrowser in constructor,
  uses custom loopback client with intercepted openBrowser wrapper
- src/index.ts: Pass openBrowser to MsalAuthenticator constructor
- test/loopback.test.ts: 16 tests covering full login flow simulation,
  landing page rendering, button enable/disable, redirects, auth code
  capture, success/error pages, XSS escaping, edge cases
- AGENTS.md: Updated architecture and auth documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use localhost instead of 127.0.0.1 in redirect URI for Entra compatibility

Azure AD treats http://localhost with any port as a valid redirect URI
for native/public client apps, but http://127.0.0.1 is not given that
exemption. This matches MSAL's default LoopbackClient behavior: listen
on 127.0.0.1 but report the redirect URI as http://localhost:{port}.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: always show account picker during browser login

Add prompt: "select_account" to acquireTokenInteractive() so Azure AD
shows the account picker instead of silently using the default/last-used
account via SSO.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show manual-close fallback when window.close() is blocked

After the auto-close countdown reaches zero, browsers often block
window.close() (e.g. after OAuth redirects). Now both the login
success page and the picker done page show a fallback message:
"If this window didn't close automatically, please close it manually."

The message appears 500ms after window.close() is attempted, replacing
the countdown text.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve manifest, tool descriptions, icons, and server instructions

Manifest:
- Upgrade to manifest_version 0.3
- Add display_name, long_description, repository, homepage, support URLs
- Add icons array with light/dark theme variants
- Add tools array with rich descriptions
- Add keywords, privacy_policies (Microsoft), compatibility

Tool descriptions:
- login: Instruct AI to proceed directly without asking user
- todo_config: Instruct AI to call directly when config needed
- auth_status: Describe as a diagnostic first step
- All tools: Richer descriptions with more context
- Input params: Add .describe() to all zod schemas

Server instructions:
- Add global instructions to McpServer guiding AI behavior
- Auto-login on auth errors, auto-config on missing list

Icons:
- Rename to icon.png (brand), icon-light.png, icon-dark.png
- Remove Windows Zone.Identifier metadata files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* remove em dash

* Add 2-minute timeout to browser login and improve retry messages

Browser login (src/auth.ts):
- Wrap acquireTokenInteractive in Promise.race with 2-minute timeout
- On timeout, close loopback server and throw descriptive error
- Falls back to device code flow (existing behavior)
- Cleanup via finally block (clearTimeout + closeServer)

Tool error messages:
- login tool: append retry hint on failure
- todo_config tool: detect timeout vs other errors, append
  contextual retry hint for the AI assistant

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: embed auth URL directly in landing page to fix Edge account picker

Instead of the landing page linking to /redirect which did a 302 to
Microsoft's auth URL, the auth URL is now embedded directly in the
sign-in button's href attribute. This ensures Edge treats the
navigation the same as MSAL's original openBrowser(authUrl) behavior,
showing the full account picker instead of pre-selecting the default
profile account.

When the auth URL is not yet available (race condition), an error page
is shown instead of a disabled button.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(vip): settle delay after TDC events + correct word-count prediction

spadmic_driver: add 500 ns/conversion settle delay after inject_tdc_events()
to prevent a subsequent disable-CTRL from catching a transient path_idle
window between back-to-back MPTDC packets.

spadmic_tdc_ref_model: fix words_per_hit to match actual MPTDC v2.3 packet
format — RAW_FEATURES=3, RAW_TIMESTAMP=2, FULL=4 — and update the formula
base from 2+1 to 3 (header + subheader + EOC). Eliminates the spurious
'word count 48 != predicted 18' warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: voice product agent with Koog AI + Spring AI MCP server

- Frontend: Next.js with AWS Transcribe auto language detection (PCM, multi-language)
- Agent: Java + Koog 0.7.3 AI agent for product search planning and summarization
- MCP Server: Java + Spring AI MCP server exposing product search tool

#17 Replacing `DotNetZip` with `System.IO.Compression` (#18)

* Replace DotNetZip with System.IO.Compression

Updated zip file handling in ShuffleDeployer and ShuffleSolutionImport to use .NET's built-in System.IO.Compression library. Removed DotNetZip references from project and package configurations. Improved file path handling with Path.Combine and added error handling for missing .cdpkg files.

* Remove extensive XML documentation from Ionic libraries

The changes streamline the `Ionic.Zip.xml` documentation by removing detailed descriptions, remarks, examples, and parameter explanations for various classes and members in the Ionic.Zip and Ionic.Zlib libraries. This includes properties and methods in classes such as `ZipEntry`, `ZipFile`, `ParallelDeflateOutputStream`, `ZlibStream`, and `CRC32`.

Additionally, the `Ionic.Zip.xml` file has been removed from the project file (`Rappen.XTB.Shuffle.csproj`), indicating that the project may no longer require this documentation or has shifted its resource management. These modifications may impact the clarity and usability of the documentation for developers relying on comprehensive guidance for implementation and troubleshooting.

* Updated dependencies

* Removed DotNetZip from nuspecs

* Minor fixes

* Update `ShuffleDeployer.cs` for traversal attacks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Updated dependencies

* Migrated to PackageReference, bug fix in `ShuffleSolutionImport.cs`

* Update NuGet package versions for Workflow and Protocol

Upgraded Microsoft.CrmSdk.Workflow to 9.0.2.60 and NuGet.Protocol to 7.3.0 to ensure compatibility and access to the latest features and fixes.

commit.message:
fix: arm TDC conv_arm before injecting cal events

The MPTDC async frontend gates start events with conv_arm_i
(mptdc_async_frontend_v2.sv:108). Without writing CSR_CTRL.conv_arm=1
to each per-axis MPTDC, cal start/stop pulses are silently ignored.

Added program_tdc_conv_arm() to both CSR and I2C drivers, called
during drive_ctrl after max_hits programming.

fix: force re-extraction when cached vuln data is not from attestation

The old Trivy-based vulnerability data has source != 'attestation'.
The cache invalidation logic now checks this field and triggers
re-extraction for any container image whose cached vulnerabilities
didn't come from a cosign attestation, ensuring the migration from
Trivy to per-arch embedded attestations runs on the next fetch even
when image digests haven't changed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): enable mono-cache inline path for closure calls

Previously, closure calls (ctx_ptr != 0) were forced to a slow path
that called jit_runtime_get_jit_entry + jit_runtime_finish_direct_call
on EVERY iteration (2 FFI crossings per call). This was because the
cached ctx_ptr was considered potentially dangling.

However, the ctx_ptr is safe for monomorphic cache hits because:
- callee_i64 match guarantees the same closure (same heap handle)
- The closure's Rc<BytecodeArray> keeps the context Rc alive
- The callee receives context via RSI (direct context slot stubs),
  not from the global TLS context cell

Now closures use the same zero-FFI mono-cache inline path as plain
functions: BA save/restore inline + direct CALL to entry point.
This eliminates ~200-300 cycles of per-call overhead.

Expected impact: closure_counter benchmark should improve significantly
(was SIGSEGV or 316x behind V8, now using the same fast path as
non-closure calls).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add comprehensive lab study guide for AKS networking

Complete LAB-GUIDE.md covering all 7 Terraform modules (00-06):
- Module 00: Foundation (VNet, subnets, NSGs)
- Module 01: Kubenet (overlay networking, route tables, pod CIDR)
- Module 02: Azure CNI (real pod IPs, NIC pre-allocation)
- Module 03: Services & ClusterIP (kube-proxy, service discovery)
- Module 04: Azure Load Balancer (backend pool = nodes, internal/external)
- Module 05: NGINX Ingress (Layer 7 pods inside cluster)
- Module 06: AGIC (direct pod routing, ARM API config)

Each module includes: learning objectives, exam relevance (AZ-104/AZ-305),
concepts overview, deploy instructions, explore & verify commands (CLI,
kubectl, Portal), graded exercises (guided/exploratory/challenge), and
key takeaways with exam tips.

checklist, knowledge gaps, and official resource links.

commit.message:
fix: wait for config commit + drain delay before test ends

Two fixes for TDC smoke test functional failures:

1. Driver now calls wait_cfg_accept() AFTER program_global_ctrl() to
   ensure the sequencer has committed the requested config to the
   active image before injecting events. Without this, events arrive
   while active_global_enable is still 0.

2. EOT handler now waits drain_timeout_ns (default 50us) before
   returning, giving the MPTDC pipeline time to produce and serialize
   packets before the monitors are killed by env.run() join_any exit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Created plans/assign-task/assign-task-plan.md with 5-phase design:
- Phase 1: RequestingParty sealed interface (api module)
- Phase 2: AssignTaskCommand and AgentTaskAssignedEvent (agentic module)
- Phase 3: Built-in handlers and registration (agentic module)
- Phase 4: TaskAwareState interface and AssignedTask record (api module)
- Phase 5: Integration tests

Phase 2: AssignTaskCommand and AgentTaskAssignedEvent with type constants (agentic module)
Phase 3: AssignTaskCommandHandler, event-sourcing handler, factory registration, controller schema registration

* Add comprehensive tests for AssignTask support

API module tests:
- RequestingPartySerializationTest: sealed interface, annotations, equality, pattern matching
- TaskAwareStateTest: AssignedTask record, TaskAwareState interface contract

Agentic module tests:

feat: use per-arch cosign attestations for SBOM and vuln data

Replace single-tag CycloneDX SBOM extraction and local Trivy scanning
with per-architecture cosign attestation extraction for SUSE registry
container images.

Changes:
- fetch_suse_registry_images.py: replace extract_sbom() with
  extract_attestations_per_arch() which uses crane manifest to enumerate
  per-arch digests, then runs cosign verify-attestation --type cyclonedx
  and --type vuln per digest using {image}@{digest} addressing.
  Vuln summary (counts + scan_date from metadata.scanFinishedOn) is
  stored directly in image_data["vulnerabilities"]. If no attestation
  is found the field is omitted and the dashboard shows N/A.
  Both sboms/ and vulns/ dirs are created on startup.

- process_vulnerabilities.py: remove Trivy scanning for registry images
  entirely. Chart aggregation now reads vuln data already populated by
  the fetch step. Trivy is kept only for GHCR images.

- generate_dashboard.py: SBOM badges now include the architecture suffix
  (e.g. "CycloneDX (amd64)") to distinguish per-arch SBOMs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent index.db bloat with auto_vacuum and startup maintenance (#378)

Enable SQLite incremental auto_vacuum so freed pages are reused for new
writes instead of accumulating on the freelist permanently. Add a
maintenance() method that runs FTS optimize, incremental_vacuum, WAL
checkpoint, and ANALYZE — time-gated to fire only on the first indexing
pass after startup (4-hour throttle), making it a complete no-op during
the 5-second auto-refresh cycles.

Root causes addressed:
- auto_vacuum=NONE caused 200 MB of permanent freelist waste (35% of DB)
- FTS5 segments never optimized during incremental indexing
- WAL never explicitly checkpointed after heavy writes

Changes:
- index_db/mod.rs: ensure_incremental_auto_vacuum() with one-time VACUUM
  migration, maintenance()/maintenance_force()/run_full_maintenance()
  with epoch-based throttling, Migration 10 for maintenance_state table
- lib.rs: reindex_search_content calls time-gated maintenance(),
  rebuild_search_content calls maintenance_force() (bypass throttle)
- 147 tests pass, zero warnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent unauthorized access to other clients' event images on login/signup screens

- Add authentication check in clientLogin.js slider initialization
- Add authentication check in clientSignup.js slider initialization
- Only display event images if user is already authenticated
- This prevents unauthenticated users from seeing other clients' events
- Both old and new clients can no longer see each other's event images on login screen

feat!: upgrade to NestJS 11

 BREAKING CHANGE: peer dependencies now require NestJS ^11. NestJS <11 is no longer supported.

 - Upgrade @nestjs/* packages to v11
 - Upgrade @nestjs/apollo to v13 with @apollo/server v5
 - Add @as-integrations/express5 peer dep (required by @nestjs/apollo@13)
 - Update ExplorerService to return InstanceWrapper[] (cqrs v11 API)
 - Update RateLimitedEventBus.bind() to accept InstanceWrapper<IEventHandler>
 - Fix PassportStrategy validate() visibility: protected -> public
 - Fix RequestUser decorator to use ExecutionContext API properly
 - Fix strict type issues in JWT/Keycloak strategies from stricter typings
 - Add uuid devDep (no longer transitively available)
